tools.name: search
tools.description: Search the tldraw Editor API spec by writing JavaScript that receives a `spec` object and returns a result. The spec contains: spec.members (all Editor methods/properties with name, kind, signature, description, category), spec.categories (category names), spec.types.shapes (focused shape type defin

tools.name: exec
tools.description:
Execute JavaScript code on a tldraw canvas. The code runs in the widget with access to the live `editor` instance, helper functions, and normal js. Use the `search` tool first to discover available Editor methods and shape types.

Each canvas has a unique `canvasId`. Omit `canvasId` to create a new

tools.name: _exec_callback
tools.description: App-only: widget calls this to resolve a pending exec request.

tools.name: _get_canvas_state
tools.description: App-only: get the latest checkpoint for a canvas by its canvasId.

tools.name: read_checkpoint
tools.description: App-only: read shapes from a checkpoint by ID.

tools.name: save_checkpoint
tools.description: App-only: save shapes to a checkpoint (from user edits). shapesJson and assetsJson must be JSON array strings.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "search" is 809 chars, far longer than this server's median (88). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Search the tldraw Editor API spec by writing JavaScript that receives a `spec` object and returns a result. The spec contains: spec.members (all Editor methods/…

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "search" appears informational but accepts parameters (code) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "exec" is 1438 chars, far longer than this server's median (88). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.
